response.justification: The candidate's profile diverges substantially from the role's mandatory requirements. C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis, falling far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows that the role demands. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience listed (only SQL/MySQL), no Windows-platform specialization, and no capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain background. The candidate's strengths are in web/full-stack work with TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, which do not substitute for the role's core technical and domain requirements.
strengths: Broad senior-level engineering experience across multiple stacks and platforms
Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development)
Excellent communication skills evidenced by workshops, talks, and teaching
concerns: C++ experience is roughly six months — far below the required 5–10 years
No SQL Server or Oracle experience and no demonstrated Windows-platform development
No capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain exposure
